current_state: Amsterdam remains the richest current municipal register row but buyer-side and public-companion support are unresolved on captured public routes.
already_tested_or_frozen_route_class: Blocked Amsterdam municipal site search plus static TenderNed shell-only retrieval; register page itself already audited for named-but-not-public DPIA/ethical-review signals.
what_does_not_count_as_progress_now: Repeating the same municipal search URLs, repeating the same static TenderNed shell probes, or restating the register page as if named assessment fields were public attachments.
what_would_count_as_real_progress: One genuinely different static official procurement, contract, tender-lot, buyer-side identifier bridge, or downloadable public assessment companion explicitly tied to the ChatAmsterdam row.
publication_delta_if_real_progress_lands: Highest-value sf09 threshold: one core municipal row moves from register-rich disclosure to exact buyer-side or public-companion support, narrowing the main Table 1B objection without widening the denominator.
current_best_use_without_new_threshold: Reuse as the exact-row-rich / buyer-side-unresolved exemplar for methods, limitations, and reviewer-response prose.
dashboard_or_figure_hook: Amsterdam blocker card; traceability-ladder caution badge; route-maturity inset.

current_state: Helsinki remains the strongest current middle state: same-buyer procurement continuity and IBM-consistent award metadata are public, but linkage is still family-level rather than exact row-specific proof.
already_tested_or_frozen_route_class: The current Hilma notice family and general register-method white paper are already captured and should be treated as a frozen family-level bridge.
what_does_not_count_as_progress_now: Rephrasing the same Hilma package as if it named the parking-chatbot page, or treating the general register white paper as a page-specific DPIA/impact-assessment companion.
what_would_count_as_real_progress: One page-specific contract, tender lot, award attachment, DPIA, impact-assessment document, or equivalent official companion explicitly tied to the parking-chatbot register row.
publication_delta_if_real_progress_lands: Second-highest sf09 threshold: Helsinki would become an exact row-linked municipal exemplar instead of a family-level comparator, sharpening the Amsterdam-versus-Helsinki contrast without changing the core row count.
current_best_use_without_new_threshold: Reuse as the family-level procurement-continuity comparator and supplier-consistency middle state.
dashboard_or_figure_hook: Traceability-ladder endpoint; family-level-linkage badge; supplier-consistency inset.

current_state: Serco already provides direct regulator-document depth for legal-basis and remedy language but remains acquisition-thin.
already_tested_or_frozen_route_class: Current local package is regulator-text-complete relative to its present role; no buyer-side procurement or public assessment companion is attached.
what_does_not_count_as_progress_now: More packaging-only prose around the ICO notice, or recaptioning regulator text as procurement visibility or supplier proof.
what_would_count_as_real_progress: One clean public buyer-side procurement, contract, supplier-chain, or public assessment companion directly tied to the Serco notice context.
publication_delta_if_real_progress_lands: Comparator-only hardening: useful if it lands, but it does not outrank Amsterdam or Helsinki for changing the main sf09 publication ceiling.
current_best_use_without_new_threshold: Reuse as the governance-depth comparator showing direct authority text without buyer-side traceability.

current_state: Chelmer already provides direct regulator reprimand depth for DPIA/process-failure wording but remains buyer-side thin and attachment-thin.
already_tested_or_frozen_route_class: Current local package is regulator-text-complete relative to its present role; the reprimand discusses DPIA but does not attach a public DPIA companion.
what_does_not_count_as_progress_now: Reusing the same reprimand wording as if it supplied supplier trace, procurement proof, or a downloadable DPIA.
what_would_count_as_real_progress: One public school procurement, supplier, public DPIA, or impact-assessment companion tied to the Chelmer row.
publication_delta_if_real_progress_lands: Comparator-only hardening: useful for school-sector governance depth, but still below Amsterdam/Helsinki in threshold value for the first LIMEN paper.
current_best_use_without_new_threshold: Reuse as the process-failure comparator with explicit assessment-gap caution.

current_state: ORION remains the low-trace official-project-page floor with no local procurement, register, supplier, or public technical/assessment companion.
already_tested_or_frozen_route_class: The current official project page is already sufficient for its floor role and should not be mistaken for a procurement-backed deployment record.
what_does_not_count_as_progress_now: Repeating the same project page or restating project framing as if it proved procurement, supplier normalization, deployment effectiveness, or legality.
what_would_count_as_real_progress: One official procurement, register, technical, or public assessment companion from direct official surfaces.
publication_delta_if_real_progress_lands: Lower-priority floor hardening: it could raise the Slovenian row above pure project-page disclosure but would still not outrank Amsterdam/Helsinki for the main sf09 threshold.
current_best_use_without_new_threshold: Reuse as the disclosure-floor anchor and multilingual/public-sector comparability floor.
dashboard_or_figure_hook: Low-rung traceability panel; project-page-only caution badge.

current_state: Denmark is a bounded governance sidecar with regulator-page legal-basis text but no buyer-side or public-companion depth.
already_tested_or_frozen_route_class: Current local package is sufficient for governance-sidecar reuse and should stay outside the default Table 1B denominator.
what_does_not_count_as_progress_now: More translation-only paraphrase or widening the regulator page into procurement-backed deployment, harm proof, or compliance certification.
what_would_count_as_real_progress: One official procurement, DPIA, impact-assessment, or linked companion document that can stay sidecar-safe without implying denominator promotion by default.
publication_delta_if_real_progress_lands: Sidecar depth only: useful for rare-language governance breadth and thesis appendix support, but not a substitute for Amsterdam/Helsinki threshold closure.
current_best_use_without_new_threshold: Reuse as a rare-language governance sidecar that demonstrates legal-basis text without buyer-side traceability.
dashboard_or_figure_hook: Jurisdiction/language map sidecar; governance-anchor caution badge.
